{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 1065 aims to close a loophole that allows credit card companies to charge extra fees on sales taxes.",
    "Credit card companies charge businesses a 'swipe fee' of usually 2 to 4%, even on taxes and tips.",
    "California merchants paid $1.7 billion in swipe fees on sales tax alone in 2023.",
    "The average profit margin for US retailers is between 3 to 9 percent.",
    "The profit margin for grocers is only 1.6%.",
    "Profit margins for Visa and MasterCard are near 50%.",
    "Visa and MasterCard effectively control over 80% of the US payment processing market.",
    "In 2021, retailers paid a total of $137.8 billion in credit and debit card processing fees, marking a 25% increase from the previous year.",
    "The impact of high swipe fees on small businesses has garnered national bipartisan concern regarding monopoly power in the US economy."
  ]
}
